source_intake_record.raw_question_text: At low temperatures, a mixture of ${ }^{3} \text{He}$ and ${ }^{4} \text{He}$ atoms form a liquid which separates into two phases: a concentrated phase (nearly pure ${ }^{3} \text{He}$), and a dilute phase (roughly $6.5 \text{\textperthousand} { }^{3} \text{He}$ for $T \text{\textless}= 0.1 \text{ K}$). The lighter ${ }^{3} \text{He}$ floats on top of the dilute phase, and ${ }^{3} \text{He}$ atoms can cross the phase boundary (see Fig. 2.23). The superfluid ${ }^{4} \text{He}$ has negligible excitation, and the thermodynamics of the dilute phase can be represented as an ideal degenerate Fermi gas of particles with density $n_{d}$ and effective mass $m^{*}\text{(}m^{*}\text{ is larger than }m_{3}\text{, the mass of the bare }{ }^{3} \text{He atom, due to the presence of the liquid }{ }^{4} \text{He, actually }m^{*}=2.4 m_{3}\text{)}$. We can crudely represent the concentrated phase by an ideal degenerate Fermi gas of density $n_{c}$ and particle mass $m_{3}$. How much heat is required to warm each phase from $T=0 \text{ K}$ to $T$?
extraction_notes: image fields contain PIL object string representations rather than file paths or URLs
force_requires_image inferred from explicit "see Fig. 2.23" and record-level vision_relevance="essential"

issues: metadata.image_paths contains PIL object string representations rather than file paths or URLs

image_dependency: 题干显式提到“see Fig. 2.23”，并描述两相边界与上下分层，存在一定图像依赖信号。
输入字段中 `requires_image=true` 且 `text_dominant=false`，说明上游将该题标为非纯文本主导。
但题干已给出主要物理设定与所需参数，最终求热量的推导看起来可主要依赖文字，因此不打过高分。
multi_step: 需要把浓相和稀相分别建模为低温简并费米气体，再分别求从 0 K 升温到 T 所需热量。
求解通常包含至少两步：先用低温费米气体热容关系，再对温度积分得到热量表达式。
答案是两个相对应的表达式集合，说明不是单一步骤的直接读取题。
verifiability: 答案为封闭形式的两个代数表达式，结果格式清晰，适合后续规则或符号匹配核验。
`answer_present=true`、`answer_type=short_text`，且原始样本标注 `answer_type` 为 `set`，目标答案边界较明确。
`registry_passed=true`，资产登记通过，说明样本基础可用性较好。

risk_flags: answer notation uses symbols like alpha_c, alpha_d, T_Fc, T_Fd that are not explicitly defined in the provided snippet
metadata.image_paths contains PIL object string representations rather than file paths or URLs

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和主图像资产均存在，核心语义支撑完整，不属于应拒绝的样本。
初步评分中 multi_step、verifiability 较高，且答案为可核验的封闭表达式，候选价值较高。

The lighter ${ }^{3} \text{He}$ floats on top of the dilute phase, and ${ }^{3} \text{He}$ atoms can cross the phase boundary (see Fig. 2.23). The superfluid ${ }^{4} \text{He}$ has negligible excitation, and the thermodynamics of the dilute phase can be represented as an ideal degenerate Fermi gas of particles with density $n_{d}$ and effective mass $m^{*}\text{(}m^{*}\text{ is larger than }m_{3}\text{, the mass of the bare }{ }^{3} \text{He atom, due to the presence of the liquid }{ }^{4} \text{He, actually }m^{*}=2.4 m_{3}\text{)}$. We can crudely represent the concentrated phase by an ideal degenerate Fermi gas of density $n_{c}$ and particle mass $m_{3}$. How much heat is required to warm each phase from $T=0 \text{ K}$ to $T$?

agent_assessment.rationale: The problem statement clearly specifies the two phases, modeling assumptions, and the quantity to compute; the figure is only a simple schematic confirming the phase arrangement and is not needed to understand the expected pair of heat expressions.

cleaning_records.decision_rationale: The question is complete and understandable on its own, the expected pair of heat expressions is materially available, and the rewrite, alignment, and solvability evidence show no serious semantic failure. The image is not needed for annotation.
